core.when: 配置布林带参数时
core.action: 使用标准布林带参数（window=20, window_dev=2）作为默认值；理解该参数假设价格服从正态分布，在常态下约 5% 时间价格会触及布林带边缘；金融市场中实际分布通常具有厚尾特征，因此价格突破布林带的频率会高于理论预期的 5%

consequence.description: 使用默认 20 期窗口和 2 倍标准差的布林带假设价格服从高斯分布，而金融市场收益率普遍存在厚尾特征，导致实际价格突破布林带的频率显著高于理论值，产生大量假突破信号

core.when: 在 A 股回测选股和下单时
core.action: 在选股时检查 close >= prev_close * 1.1（涨停）则过滤或标记为不可买入；在下单时检查订单价格 >= prev_close * 1.1 则标记为涨停价订单不可成交；同理检查跌停价订单（close <= prev_close * 0.9）

consequence.description: 未过滤涨停股时，动量策略会假设可在涨停价全额买入，系统性高估回测收益率；而未处理跌停卖单会导致实盘中部分卖单无法按预期价格成交

core.action: Configure stop-loss without accounting for T+1 settlement preventing same-day liquidation—when limit-down occurs, stop-loss sell orders queue for next day and the stock may gap down further, causing realized losses to far exceed the -30% threshold

consequence.description: Stop-loss at -30% becomes ineffective in A-share limit-down scenarios because T+1 settlement prevents same-day liquidation. core.when: Implementing position sizing with liquidity filters for A-share trading
core.action: Round calculated position sizes to 100-share multiples (1手 = 100 shares minimum) and handle suspension periods—the system calculates continuous position amounts but A-share trading requires discrete lot sizes, causing rounding errors to accumulate over many trades

consequence.description: Liquidity filters (300M CNY turnover, 2% turnover rate, 2x volume breakthrough) combined with A-share lot size constraints (1手 = 100 shares minimum) create a risk cascade: position sizes calculated as continuous amounts must round to 100-share multiples, rounding errors accumulate over many trades, and during suspension periods the calculated position cannot be adjusted at all

core.when: Using short-term technical signals (price proximity filters) with adjusted prices for entry timing
core.action: Use backward-adjusted (qfq) prices for short-term technical entry analysis—forward-adjusted (hfq) prices bake dividends and splits into historical data, making MA distance calculations inaccurate for entry timing (a stock appearing 10% below MA in hfq may be 12% below in qfq)

consequence.description: Forward-adjusted prices distort short-term technical entry signals because dividends and splits are already baked into historical prices. The 'distance from MA' calculation becomes inaccurate for entry timing—a stock appearing 10% below its MA in hfq prices might actually be 12% below in qfq prices, causing systematic entry timing errors

core.when: Enabling rich_mode=True with tiered position sizing (position_pct = 20%/50%/100%)
core.action: Enable rich_mode=True without understanding it bypasses all capital constraints—rich_mode auto-injects unlimited money when capital is insufficient, making the tiered position sizing protective mechanism entirely ineffective and allowing strategies to deploy theoretically unlimited capital

consequence.description: rich_mode=True bypasses tiered position sizing safeguards by auto-injecting unlimited money when capital is insufficient, making the protective mechanism (preventing over-commitment) never trigger. A strategy could theoretically deploy 1000% of capital because rich_mode always provides more, making backtest risk assessment fundamentally unreliable for live trading

core.when: Building stock price prediction models using Linear Regression with StandardScaler
core.action: Use Linear Regression with StandardScaler on raw financial time series data without verifying model assumptions—Linear Regression assumes linear relationships, normality, independence, and no multicollinearity, all of which are violated in financial data

consequence.description: Linear Regression with StandardScaler assumes linear relationships, normality, independence, and no multicollinearity—assumptions that fail in real financial markets where relationships are non-linear, distributions exhibit heavy tails, and features show strong autocorrelation and multicollinearity, leading to unreliable predictions and systematic pricing errors

core.when: Implementing cost models for order execution in backtesting
core.action: Assume a fixed 0.1% slippage applies uniformly to all order sizes—the framework uses fixed slippage that does not account for volume-based market impact, causing large positions to appear more executable than they would be in live trading

consequence.description: Fixed 0.1% slippage assumes constant market impact regardless of order size. Large orders in illiquid stocks will have significantly higher slippage (0.5-2% in real markets), causing backtests to systematically overestimate execution quality and show strategies as more profitable than realizable in live trading

core.when: Calculating expected transaction costs for orders of varying sizes in illiquid markets
core.action: Implement volume-weighted slippage model: use 0.1-0.5% slippage for small orders (< 1% of average daily volume), 0.5-2% slippage for large orders (> 1% ADV), and verify the slippage estimate matches actual market conditions for the target stock's liquidity profile

consequence.description: Volume-weighted slippage (0.1-0.5% for small orders, 0.5-2% for large orders) better reflects real market impact. Without this, backtests systematically underestimate transaction costs for large positions in illiquid stocks, making strategies appear more profitable than achievable in live execution
